name: Steve
claudeRead: Steve is one session from the attempt. The portfolio is live, the financial runway is confirmed, and the first pitch is drafted. Alex knows the plan. The main risk has shifted from preparation to nerve: everything that can be done in advance is done. 'The Perfectionist' made its last stand - argued for one more revision before sending. It got named. The date held. That's new. The unresolved thing is sleep, and what story Steve will tell himself if the first response is silence.
progression:
Steve started 2026 with very little in place beyond a vague intention: stop drifting and actually make the move to work that fits. Years in a stable corporate job that paid well but left him hollow had produced a familiar loop - endure the week, spend the weekend recovering, repeat. The early sessions were about establishing a baseline. Deep work, movement, and morning routine became the three anchors. They held. The graveyard began filling: Sunday Dread, The Hoarder, Twitter Spiral. Things that had run quietly for years lost their cover once they had names.

By March, the destination was no longer vague. A real first target had been identified - a specific potential client, a specific kind of work, a specific next step that was not just planning to plan. Preparation shifted from diffuse effort into something concrete: portfolio updated and live, financial runway confirmed, pitch drafted. The sessions started feeling different - more like contact with something real than stockpiling against a formless threat. 'The Perfectionist' made its move in early April, arguing for one more revision before sending. It got named. The date held. That was new.

What is live now is less about readiness than nerve. Everything is in place. The main unknown going into the attempt is whether Steve will show up to it depleted. Sleep is the deficit that has run under the surface all year - compensated for by motivation, masked by consistency, but still there. The attempt is in days. The pattern to watch is not avoidance but the quiet erosion that happens when pressure is high and recovery is last in line.

air.sliderLabel: More aware than acting - noticing patterns without changing habits yet

fire.sliderLabel: Motivation building - purpose clearer but still running on obligation more than drive

water.sliderLabel: Some aliveness - deep work and movement reliable, rest of life still muted

earth.sliderLabel: Reasonably grounded - anxiety present but not dominating the decisions

allies.name: Alex
allies.desc: Close friend since the early days. Reliable, honest, shows up when it actually matters.
allies.why: Knows the full story - not just the highlight reel.

allies.name: Old Mentor
allies.desc: Someone who made a similar leap a decade ago and navigated harder seasons. Gives perspective without making it about himself.
allies.why: Cuts through the noise and finds what actually matters.
allies.corruption: Only reached out when the situation gets bad enough - waits too long.

allies.name: Focused Work Block
allies.desc: 90 minutes with a specific intention, phone in another room, nothing else open.
allies.why: Reliable reset. Costs only time and attention.

allies.name: Solo Walk
allies.desc: One hour with no agenda, no phone. Returns with more energy than it started with.
allies.why: Works even when motivation is near zero.
allies.corruption: Gets cut exactly when pressure is highest - the moment it would help most.

allies.name: Music
allies.desc: Headphones on, no performance, no audience. Just sound and whatever is in front of me.
allies.why: Quiets the mental noise that planning cannot reach.

allies.name: Low-Stakes Conversations
allies.desc: Time with people where nothing critical is on the line. Easy rhythm, no pressure.
allies.why: Restorative in a way that productive sessions are not.

allies.name: The Problem Solver
allies.desc: Track record of figuring things out under uncertainty is real.
allies.why: Counters the imposter voice with evidence rather than reassurance.

allies.name: The Long Game
allies.desc: Slow progress toward the right thing beats fast progress toward the wrong one.
allies.why: Useful counter to urgency and comparison spirals.

allies.name: The Body Knows
allies.desc: The aliveness felt doing the right work is data, not coincidence.
allies.why: The qualitative difference between right work and wrong is the clearest signal available.

mainQuest.title: Defeat the Ender Dragon
mainQuest.description: Leave the job that no longer fits and make the leap to work that actually matters. The dragon is the career move that has been circling for two years - the one that gets named and delayed every time it gets close. The prep is done. What's left is going.
mainQuest.whyItMatters: Every year stayed in the wrong place is another year the right one gets harder to find. The drift is not neutral - it compounds. If the dragon keeps circling, everything else feels provisional.
mainQuest.doneWhen: First client signed, notice handed in, or the project live. Standing on the other side having actually done it - not planned it, not prepared for it. Done.

nextSteps.text: Send the first pitch to the identified target - the prep is done, this is the step
nextSteps.doneWhen: Pitch sent to a real person, not saved as a draft

nextSteps.text: Confirm the financial runway covers at least three months without the current income
nextSteps.doneWhen: Number checked, not estimated - actual runway confirmed

nextSteps.text: Tell one more person about the plan - someone whose reaction will matter
nextSteps.doneWhen: Said out loud to someone other than Alex

sideQuests.title: Get Settled and Stable
sideQuests.description: Moved to a new flat at the start of the year. It is functional but not yet a working environment - workspace improvised, storage chaotic, nothing quite where it should be.
sideQuests.whyItMatters: Hard to build anything serious from an unstable base. The environment shapes the thinking.
sideQuests.doneWhen: Dedicated workspace sorted, storage working, the flat feels like a home rather than a temporary situation.
sideQuests.nextStep: Sort the overflow area this week - it has been sitting there since the move.

sideQuests.title: Get Properly Equipped for the Leap
sideQuests.description: Portfolio updated and live. Financial runway confirmed. First pitch drafted and ready to send. The groundwork before the big attempt.
sideQuests.whyItMatters: Showing up to the attempt underprepared is a different kind of failure. The leap needs the right gear.
sideQuests.doneWhen: All three: portfolio live, runway confirmed, one real pitch actually sent - not drafted, sent.
sideQuests.nextStep: Send the pitch - the preparation is at 80%, the last 20% is doing the thing.

sideQuests.title: Explore 12 New Things This Year
sideQuests.description: One genuinely new thing per month - a place, a skill, a conversation, an environment outside the usual radius. Variety and curiosity over efficiency.
sideQuests.whyItMatters: Exploration generates unexpected insight and breaks tunnel vision about what is possible.
sideQuests.doneWhen: 12 different experiences with at least one reflection note from each.
sideQuests.nextStep: Find one genuinely new thing this month before moving to the next.

sideQuests.title: Organise the Whole Setup
sideQuests.description: Every part of life running in background chaos - digital files, physical space, finances - gets a clear place and a working system.
sideQuests.whyItMatters: Chaos in the environment creates chaos in decision-making. The two are not separate.
sideQuests.doneWhen: Every category has a place. Overflow pile gone. Nothing is lost in the mess.
sideQuests.nextStep: Clear the overflow pile - it has been sitting there for a month.

sideQuests.title: Build Three Time-Saving Systems
sideQuests.description: Finance tracking, email management, and one more. Three systems that run without constant manual effort - set up once, save time indefinitely.
sideQuests.whyItMatters: Automation creates leverage. Effort compounds instead of repeating.
sideQuests.doneWhen: Three systems running reliably without manual intervention every week.
sideQuests.nextStep: Map out the first system this week - not build it, just design what it needs to do.

completedQuests.title: Survive the First Month
completedQuests.howResolved: Built the foundation with very little in place. Made it through with no major setbacks. The three-month consistency run started there.

needs.name: Connection
needs.description: Real relationships where the full picture is known. Good with a few, absent with most.

needs.name: Rest
needs.description: Recovery time not immediately colonised by tasks or noise. Significant deficit.

needs.name: Autonomy
needs.description: Control over what to work on and when. Better than it was.

needs.name: Recognition
needs.description: Feeling that the work and effort are seen. The current environment is neutral at best.

values.name: Authenticity
values.description: Living in the way that actually fits, not the version others expect or the one that felt safe to settle into.

values.name: Family
values.description: Being present and reliable for the people who matter most.

values.name: Health
values.description: Physical and mental baseline as a non-negotiable foundation, not the last thing on the list.

values.name: Growth
values.description: Getting better at the things that actually matter.

values.name: Financial Security
values.description: Enough stability to make genuine choices without anxiety driving the decision.

skills.name: Set Boundaries
skills.description: Decline requests that conflict with the main quest without over-explaining or apologising.
skills.whyItMatters: Without this, time and energy get consumed by everyone else's priorities.

skills.name: Ask For Help
skills.description: Reach out before getting lost in a problem alone. State the actual need, not a softened version of it.
skills.whyItMatters: Directly determines how fast the main quest actually progresses.

skills.name: Stay Consistent
skills.description: Show up even when motivation is absent. The anchor habits hold regardless of how the week feels.
skills.whyItMatters: Deep work, movement, and morning routine all depend on this.

skills.name: Deep Focus
skills.description: Sustain attention on one thing long enough for real progress to happen. No phone, no tabs, one thing.
skills.whyItMatters: The capability that produces meaningful output.

skills.name: Planning
skills.description: Break big goals into clear next steps without over-engineering the system.
skills.whyItMatters: In high-uncertainty territory, clear direction is the primary signal.

skills.name: Adaptability
skills.description: Read the situation, adjust the approach, keep moving forward.
skills.whyItMatters: Foundation of making progress when conditions are not ideal.

bosses.name: The Ender Dragon
bosses.desc: The central force that frames every shortcoming as evidence of fundamental under-readiness for the leap. Feels like an accurate read of the situation. It isn't.
bosses.whyBoss: Every other enemy is this one in a different form. Procrastination, Self-Doubt, and Comparison all run on the same engine: the belief that the jump to better work isn't possible yet, or not for someone like Steve.
bosses.shortTermBenefit: Keeps standards high. Feels like appropriate caution before a high-stakes commitment.
bosses.origin: Formed in an environment where being under-prepared was the primary risk. Caution treated as virtue long after the context changed.

vulnerabilities.title: Named in real time
vulnerabilities.desc: Loses power the moment it is caught mid-loop. Calling it out loud creates enough distance to choose differently.

vulnerabilities.title: Evidence on the table
vulnerabilities.desc: Thrives on selective memory. A written list of what has actually gone well - just facts - starves it of its operating data.

vulnerabilities.title: Starting before ready
vulnerabilities.desc: Main lever is the gap before beginning. Starting small collapses the gap before the pattern can fill it with objections.

minions.name: PROCRASTINATION
minions.desc: Masquerades as preparation. Rearranges the to-do list instead of starting the actual thing. Feels like productivity.

minions.name: SELF-DOUBT
minions.desc: Quiet voice that says others are more capable, more ready, more legitimate. Speaks in first person so it sounds like truth.

minions.name: COMPARISON TRAP
minions.desc: Measures a messy middle against someone else's polished highlight reel. Always finds the comparison unflattering.

current.name: LATE-NIGHT SCROLL HOARDING
current.desc: Reliably ends worse than it started. Feels like rest but leaves the next day already depleted before it begins.
current.trigger: Evening wind-down, especially after a difficult or draining day
current.shortTermBenefit: Decompression that asks nothing. Feels like a reward without any vulnerability.
current.origin: Avoidance of the quiet where unprocessed feelings from the day tend to surface.

current.name: OBLIGATION YES
current.desc: Saying yes to avoid the discomfort of declining. Fills time with others' priorities instead of the main quest.
current.trigger: Any request that feels like it expects a yes by default
current.shortTermBenefit: Avoids conflict and the guilt of letting someone down. Feels like being a good person.
current.origin: Keeping others comfortable felt safer than asserting what actually matters.

current.name: SKIP MOVEMENT WHEN STRESSED
current.desc: Cutting the one habit that would most help at the moments of highest pressure. Always justified in the moment.
current.trigger: High external pressure, low energy, feeling behind on everything
current.shortTermBenefit: Creates the illusion of more work time. Feels like a sensible trade-off.
current.origin: Anxiety framing physical recovery as optional when output feels most urgent.

current.name: I AM NOT CAPABLE ENOUGH
current.belief: "I'm not capable enough to go after the things I actually want."
current.desc: Shows up before high-stakes attempts. Reframes the learning curve as permanent evidence of inadequacy.
current.shortTermBenefit: Keeps ambition small and safe from the humiliation of public failure.
current.origin: Environments where being underprepared was the primary risk. Performance over presence.

current.name: IF I CAN'T DO IT PERFECTLY THERE IS NO POINT
current.belief: "If I can't do it perfectly, there's no point starting."
current.desc: Main engine of procrastination. Creates an impossible standard that ensures nothing gets finished on time.
current.shortTermBenefit: The unstarted thing can always be imagined as flawless. Starting would reveal the gaps.
current.origin: Environments where only exceptional output was noticed. Ordinary effort was invisible.

current.name: I ALWAYS FALL SHORT BEFORE THE GOOD STUFF
current.belief: "I always fall short right before the good stuff happens."
current.desc: Selective memory that catalogues every failure and dismisses successful runs as luck or a fluke.
current.shortTermBenefit: Low expectations mean any progress feels like a win. Feels like protection against disappointment.
current.origin: Reinforced by a period where several genuine attempts ended just before the payoff.

graveyard.name: SUNDAY DREAD
graveyard.howDefeated: Lost its footing once Steve stopped pretending the corporate job was the permanent plan. Without the fiction to protect, there was nothing to dread waiting for Monday.

graveyard.name: THE HOARDER
graveyard.howDefeated: Named it properly for the first time. Hoarding disguised as being organised. Once it had a name it stopped working as cover.

graveyard.name: TWITTER SPIRAL
graveyard.howDefeated: Deleted the app after noticing the pattern: open it feeling okay, close it feeling worse. Every time.

activities.name: Deep Work
activities.description: Focused work sessions with no distractions. Phone elsewhere, one thing at a time, no multitasking.
activities.whyItMatters: The meaningful progress only happens here.

activities.name: Movement
activities.description: Any intentional physical activity. Running, lifting, walking with purpose.
activities.whyItMatters: Body and mind are not separate. Energy levels follow this.

subActivities.name: Running
subActivities.description: Any sustained cardio - running, cycling, brisk walking. Gets the body moving and clears the head.

subActivities.name: Strength
subActivities.description: Resistance training, weights, or bodyweight work. Effort that builds rather than just burns.

activities.name: Morning Routine
activities.description: Consistent start to the day before the world gets loud.
activities.whyItMatters: Sets the tone. Everything downstream is easier.

activities.name: Weekly Actions
activities.description: Concrete steps toward the career leap this week: outreach sent, conversations had, portfolio updated, applications submitted. The actual moves, not the preparation for the moves.
activities.whyItMatters: Weekly consistency on the real thing matters more than any individual breakthrough.

achievements.title: No Setbacks Run
achievements.description: Three months without a major setback - the first time maintaining this for this long. Feb 14, 2026.

achievements.title: The Organiser
achievements.description: Sorted every category of the workspace backlog in a single sustained session. Labels and all. Jan 20, 2026.

achievements.title: Signal Over Noise
achievements.description: Noticed the pattern: open it feeling okay, close it feeling worse. Every time. Deleted the app. Nov 2025.

achievements.title: The Borrowed Axe
achievements.description: Asked Old Mentor for advice on the career move directly - first time without pretending it was a casual catch-up. Mar 2026.

achievements.title: Failure Is Data
achievements.description: Stopped seeing setbacks as proof of incompetence. They are information, not verdicts. Feb 2026.

classes.name: Builder
classes.description: Drawn to making things. Finds meaning in the act of building itself, not just the finished result.

classes.name: Explorer
classes.description: Curious about new territory and persistent when the destination actually matters. Learning to tell the difference between exploration and avoidance.

keyQuestions.question: If the first attempt goes badly, what story will you tell yourself - and is that story already written?
What would it mean to show up rested rather than just prepared?
When the obligation to others feels automatic, what is the cost you are not counting?

openThreads.thread: Whether sleep deficit going into the attempt is being minimised or genuinely managed
openThreads.whyItMatters: Showing up depleted is a different kind of under-ready that preparation cannot fix

openThreads.thread: Whether Obligation Yes will resurface once the main quest pressure lifts
openThreads.whyItMatters: It stayed quiet during the prep phase - high focus may have masked it, not resolved it

entries.title: One Session Before the Attempt
events: Portfolio went live - every piece of work visible in one place for the first time
Alex knows about the plan - first time saying it out loud to someone whose opinion actually matters
insights: Rest deficit showed up in the prep session - had to stop early when focus dropped. Not ignoring it this time.
Naming the date out loud to Alex made 'Procrastination' audible - it argued against the specific deadline, not the concept
tensions: Rest is still the real unknown - showing up prepared but depleted is a different kind of under-ready

entries.title: Everything Ready, Date Set
events: Completed the full preparation phase - portfolio live, runway confirmed, pitch drafted
Date set for the first outreach - told Alex, not just a plan anymore
insights: 'The Perfectionist' tried to delay with one more revision - noticed it and held the date anyway
This session felt different - building toward something specific rather than staying busy to feel less anxious about standing still
tensions: Whether the date will hold or become another thing that moves when the week gets hard

entries.title: Two Hours That Actually Landed
events: Two uninterrupted hours on the main project - first time this year without the phone interrupting
insights: Felt different from normal sessions - more like contact with something real than ticking boxes
tensions: Whether the deep work sessions are real direction or just productive-feeling avoidance of the actual step

entries.title: Asked for Help Instead of Grinding Alone
events: Asked Old Mentor for advice directly - first time without framing it as a casual catch-up
insights: 'Self-Doubt' reframed asking for help as weakness - noticed it mid-conversation this time instead of only in retrospect
tensions: Constraints shortening the timeline in ways that might force decisions before they feel ready

entries.title: Three Months Without a Setback
events: Three months without a major setback - marked it quietly, told Alex
insights: Staying consistent required a quiet decision to stop fighting battles that did not need fighting
tensions: Rest still a deficit - sessions feel productive but not restorative

Deep Work: 5h this week despite high external pressure

Morning Routine: 5 of 7 days - streak rebuilding after skip week
